key_insight: Thermal spraying can deposit durable polymer and polymer-composite coatings with tunable protection and functionality on a wide range of substrates.
engineering_relevance: These coatings can improve corrosion resistance, wear behavior, hydrophobicity, and biocompatibility for advanced surface engineering in industrial and biomedical components.
method: Surveyed major thermal spray routes for polymers: flame spraying, HVOF, plasma spraying, and cold spraying.
Compared common polymers and composites including PE, PA, PI, PU, PEEK, PET, and fluoropolymers.
Reviewed relationships among process choice, microstructure, and coating performance across applications.
evidence_signal: The review synthesizes published findings rather than reporting new experiments, focusing on deposition methods, coating properties, and application outcomes.
watch_item: Polymer thermal spraying can be limited by substrate heat sensitivity and process-dependent degradation or adhesion issues that must be managed carefully.
paper_title: A Review - Thermal spraying on Polymers
